mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month change and 20-day SPX forward returns is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% imposes restrictive financial conditions that raise discount rates and corporate debt service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During triggered periods, the average 60-day SPX forward return is positive or the hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) and deceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicate a positive supply-driven expansion in real consumption that boosts corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of instances or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals an inflection toward recessionary earnings contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes with the trigger active, the average 60-day SPX forward return is positive or the negative return hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) predict negative demand spillovers and inventory destocking that reduce corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases or generate a mean return that is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in new private housing starts (HOUST) leads construction activity and durable goods demand, causing cyclical equities to reprice higher and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST month-over-month change and 5-day SPX forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth exceeding 2% annualized triggers upward repricing of cyclical earnings expectations as the economy avoids stagnation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered quarters fail to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of instances or the mean excess return over cash is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 4.5%, a concurrent month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate is interpreted as proactive monetary insurance rather than crisis response, jointly compressing the equity risk premium and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the average 5-day SPX forward return is negative or the positive return hit rate is below 55%.
